cards.prompt: String is immutable: s += "c" does not mutate the object, it creates a new String and re-points s, so the alias t still holds the original "ab" and ReferenceEquals(s, t) is False
cards.code: using System; string s = "ab"; string t = s; s += "c"; Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(s, t)); Console.WriteLine(t);

cards.prompt: A method that appears to modify a string returns a new String object: a.Replace("l", "L") leaves a as "hello" and returns a distinct object b "heLLo", so ReferenceEquals(a, b) is False
cards.code: using System; string a = "hello"; string b = a.Replace("l", "L"); Console.WriteLine(a); Console.WriteLine(b); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(a, b));

cards.prompt: ToUpper allocates a new String each call: s stays "cat", u is "CAT", and two separate ToUpper() calls yield different objects so ReferenceEquals(s, s.ToUpper()) is False, printing cat CAT False
cards.code: using System; string s = "cat"; string u = s.ToUpper(); Console.WriteLine($"{s} {u} {object.ReferenceEquals(s, s.ToUpper())}");
